behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation/composition layer -> summary-generator input. Token "includes data retrieved from all three sources" indicates cross-module wiring, not single-function logic. Rejected Unit because this behavior is about combined handoff, not isolated parsing. Rejected System because in-process stubs for the three sources can reproduce missing-source wiring bugs without real network/OS. Rejected Agentic because it validates payload passed to generation, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel data for each source; run one report build; assert the exact payload sent into summary-generation contains all three source sections/fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience across module boundaries: source adapter failure propagation plus report assembly behavior. Token "one data source is unavailable ... rather than aborting" implies orchestrator + renderer interaction. Rejected Unit because the bug typically appears when error objects/statuses cross modules. Rejected System because adapter failure can be simulated in-process (exception/timeout stub) and still catch fallback-note logic. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Use real report orchestration with three source adapters where one adapter deterministically fails; verify run completes, report contains `data unavailable` for only that section, and other sections still render.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Token "accurately represents" is output-quality semantics, not routing. A stubbed summarizer would hide misrepresentation/hallucination failures. This behavior depends on real model/generation behavior and can vary across runs. Rejected Integration because deterministic fixtures can only test wrapper plumbing, not generation correctness. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic judgment step is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real summary-generation component on fixed input fixtures with known key values; assert the produced narrative preserves those values and does not invert/omit critical metrics, using semantic checks tolerant to phrasing variation.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery path. Token "reaches the configured recipient's inbox" requires real email transport/inbox behavior (network/service integration), which mocks can falsely pass. Rejected Integration because in-process fakes cannot prove end-to-end inbox delivery. Considered Agentic but rejected: behavior text checks delivery completion, not summary-quality/model-decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end against reachable source endpoints/test doubles plus a real email delivery path to a test mailbox; assert message arrives in configured inbox with expected report structure and recipient addressing.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff across modules/runs: delivery failure handling -> local persistence -> retry loader on subsequent run. Token "saved to a local file ... retried on the next scheduled run" indicates cross-module state propagation. Rejected Unit because this is not one function; it spans producer, persistence, and retry logic. Rejected System because deterministic send-failure simulation plus real local filesystem/temp path is sufficient to expose fallback/retry bugs without real network outage or OS scheduler.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator twice with shared temp persistence path: first run forces email adapter failure and asserts report file is written; second run simulates next scheduled invocation with email adapter healthy and asserts persisted report is retried and file/state is cleared/marked sent.
